title: marattiaceae
intro: The Marattiaceae rank refers to a family of ferns that consists of approximately 30 genera and 400 species. This family is characterized by its primitive features and simple, often small, leaves. Marattiaceae ferns are typically terrestrial and found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, often in shaded environments. They play an important role in ecosystems, serving as food sources and habitat providers for various animals.
